title: Image-Layered World Model
description: Third-person video decomposition for reasoning and control
summary: Third-person video generation can be more useful if it separates persistent scene state, object/contact dynamics, and robot/ego dynamics instead of treating video as flat pixels.
asset_caption: Persistent scene, object/contact, and robot/ego layers.
details: WEM is the closest current conceptual neighbor: it separates instruction-agnostic world evolution from instruction-conditioned robot ego dynamics for long-horizon navigation-manipulation rollout.
The project delta should be a finer manipulation decomposition: scene/background layer, object/contact transition layer, and robot/ego action layer rather than only world versus ego.
Target outputs: data augmentation, reward model, and inverse dynamics model.

IDM framing: treat inverse dynamics as the LfD / LfO bridge. Offline, it infers missing robot actions from observation-only video or state transitions, turning videos into usable state-action demonstrations. Online, the same mapping becomes a low-latency vision-conditioned teleoperation / shared-control layer.

Real-time nuance: not all LfD is teleoperation; it becomes vision-based teleoperation when the human is still in the loop and visual feedback plus IDM / retargeting maps the intended next state into immediate robot commands.
Robot layer should support robot priors at generation time: either a manipulator URDF or a single robot-arm image as the robot-condition input.
Immediate work: move from survey into layer-generation probes: first validate whether visual decomposition can produce reusable layer assets for robot scenes, not only clean poster-style edits.

Test track 1: Qwen-Image-Layered. Use poster-like images and robot manipulation stills to generate RGBA layers, then score recomposition error, duplicate or missing elements, layer purity, and whether scene/background, object/contact, and robot/ego regions are separable.

Test track 2: ChatGPT + Photoshop PSD pipeline. Prompt an image model to split each visual element into fixed-position white-background images, then use Photoshop scripting/actions to remove white, keep one layer per element, and export a PSD for inspection.

Agentic system shape: decomposition agent -> layer QA / recomposition agent -> PSD assembly agent -> manipulation relabel agent that maps visual layers into scene/background, object-contact, and robot/ego action layers.
risks_decisions: Layered model scope should move beyond visual decomposition toward state-aware scene / object-contact / robot-ego dynamics.
Object/contact state is the bridge between WEM's world and ego; if ignored, generated videos can look stable while failing manipulation physics.
Evaluation target: data augmentation quality, reward-model usefulness, IDM recoverability, real-time teleop controllability, and long-horizon phase consistency.
Qwen / poster-style layer separation may produce editable design layers but not manipulation semantics; v1 must explicitly check scene/background, object/contact, and robot/ego separability.
White-background prompt + Photoshop composition can create duplicate or missing elements; require reconstruction QA before using layers as training or evaluation data.
